rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical trifluoromethylation/cyclization of beta,gamma-alkenyl oximes with CF3SO2Na to give CF3-substituted isoxazolines
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) to generate CF3 radical (with loss of SO2); second anodic oxidation of the C-centered radical adduct to a carbocation that cyclizes; cathodic proton reduction to H2 balances charge

key_intermediates.name: CF3SO2 radical (A) from anodic oxidation of CF3SO2-
CF3 radical (B) after loss of SO2
C-centered radical adduct on alkenyl oxime (C)
Carbocation intermediate (D) after second anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- by one electron to give CF3SO2 radical, which loses SO2 to form CF3 radical; CF3 radical adds to the alkene of the beta,gamma-alkenyl oxime to give a carbon radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that undergoes intramolecular O-cyclization by the oxime hydroxyl to deliver the isoxazoline; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: 2.0 equiv almost completely suppressed formation of 3aa, indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 2.0 equiv suppressed the reaction (trace 3aa), supporting radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3aa dropped to <10%; CF3-DPE adduct 7 isolated in 55%, providing strong evidence for a free CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: Cyclopropyl-bearing alkenyl oxime 8
probe_reagents.observation: Neither cyclic nitrone 9 nor ring-opened product 10 was observed (inconclusive radical clock)

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO2Na (2a) CV
probe_reagents.observation: Irreversible oxidation peak at 1.701 V, consistent with anodic generation of CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: Alkenyl oxime 1a CV
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 2.243 V (higher than 2a); mixture 1a+2a shows onset at 1.947 V with increased current, suggesting CF3SO2- is oxidized first

notes: Radical clock with cyclopropyl substrate 8 was inconclusive (no nitrone 9 nor ring-opened 10 detected). Mechanism proposes radical-polar crossover (radical addition then anodic oxidation to cation) rather than direct radical cyclization; alternative all-radical cyclization pathway is not explicitly ruled out.
